meta.description: US county political risk scores for AI/data center development. Forward-looking indicator of likelihood of future restrictions.

score_descriptions.1: Strong political support for data center development. Pro-DC governing bodies, economic development backing, and/or significant incentive programs. No documented organized opposition.
score_descriptions.2: Generally favorable environment with minor isolated concerns. Some community discussion but no organized opposition. Incentive programs or economic development support present.
score_descriptions.3: Mixed signals. Some documented political concern, environmental group activity, or official infrastructure worry. Worth monitoring but no active campaign against development.
score_descriptions.4: Elevated risk. Organized opposition, public hearings with documented resistance, draft ordinances, or sustained media coverage of community campaigns. Restrictions are possible.
score_descriptions.5: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.

meta.coverage_note: Only counties with documented public evidence are scored. Unscored counties have insufficient data — they are NOT assumed to be favorable.

scoring_model.description: Score = clamp(1, 5, round(1.0 + Σ(signal_weight))) where signal weights are summed per county. Negative weights reduce the base risk. Score is always rounded to nearest integer and clamped to [1,5]. If no signals are documented, the county is NOT scored (confidence=none).

confidence_thresholds.high: 3+ independent documented signals from official sources
confidence_thresholds.medium: 1-2 documented signals from official or well-documented news sources
confidence_thresholds.low: Single signal from news coverage only; official confirmation pending

scores.name: Marshall County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Marshall County enacted a ban on hyperscale data centers after sustained organized opposition. Strong anti-DC political environment remains.

signals.label: Ban Enacted
signals.description: County commissioners enacted an ordinance banning hyperscale data centers (facilities over certain MW thresholds) following sustained community opposition.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Marshall County residents organized formal opposition campaign against proposed hyperscale facilities, citing noise, water, and infrastructure concerns.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Multiple public hearings drew significant opposition testimony from residents, agricultural interests, and environmental advocates.

scores.name: Cass County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Cass County enacted prohibitive restrictions after organized rural opposition. Hostile political environment for data center development.

signals.label: Ban Enacted
signals.description: Cass County adopted restrictions effectively prohibiting large data center development in agricultural and residential zones.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Rural community opposition organized against proposed data center development, citing changes to agricultural character.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Public hearings on proposed facilities generated significant documented opposition.

scores.name: Hood River County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Hood River County enacted a comprehensive data center ban. Environmental, agricultural, and scenic preservation groups drove organized opposition.

signals.label: Ban Enacted
signals.description: Hood River County adopted a comprehensive ban on new data center development citing water, agricultural land, and scenic corridor concerns. The ban is among the most restrictive in the US.

signals.label: Environmental Group Opposition
signals.description: Multiple environmental and agricultural preservation groups actively supported the ban citing Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area integrity.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County planning commission hearings on data center proposals drew extensive documented public opposition.

signals.label: Active Advocacy Group
signals.description: Local advocacy groups campaigned for permanent prohibition on data center development to protect agricultural land and scenic corridor.

scores.name: Providence County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Rhode Island enacted restrictive data center legislation following documented public and environmental opposition.

signals.label: Ban Enacted
signals.description: Rhode Island legislation established significant restrictions on new data center development citing grid reliability, carbon emissions, and water consumption concerns.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: State legislature hearings on data center impacts drew documented public testimony opposing expansion of facilities.

signals.label: Environmental Group Opposition
signals.description: Rhode Island environmental organizations documented opposition to data center electricity consumption and carbon impact.

scores.name: Fauquier County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Fauquier County enacted a moratorium on data center rezonings after organized rural preservation campaigns. Political environment remains highly active and opposed to further development.

signals.label: Moratorium Enacted
signals.description: Board of Supervisors enacted a temporary moratorium on data center rezoning applications (September 2023) while updating Comprehensive Plan to address rural impact concerns.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Rural preservation groups organized sustained campaign opposing data center development in agricultural and rural buffer zones, citing viewshed, noise, and rural character.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Multiple public hearings on data center rezonings drew documented significant community opposition testimony.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Sustained local and regional media coverage documented community opposition to proposed rural data center developments.

scores.name: Orange County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Orange County has active organized opposition with moratorium proposals and extensive public hearings. Data center development highly contentious.

signals.label: Moratorium Proposed
signals.description: Orange County commissioners considered moratorium proposals on data center development following documented community opposition to large-scale facilities in rural and semi-rural areas.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Organized resident groups sustained campaign opposing data center development, citing water use, noise, light pollution, and rural character impacts.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Planning board hearings drew extensive documented opposition testimony from residents, environmental advocates, and preservation groups.

signals.label: Active Advocacy Group
signals.description: Multiple advocacy groups actively organized opposition to data center development in Orange County.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Extensive coverage in local and Triangle-area media documenting organized opposition.

scores.name: Chatham County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Chatham County has documented organized opposition, formal planning studies, and Jordan Lake watershed concerns driving active political resistance to data center development.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Chatham County planning hearings on proposed large data center campuses near Pittsboro drew documented organized opposition citing water use, power demand, and rural character impacts.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Residents and advocacy groups organized sustained campaign opposing data center development in Chatham County's research corridor and rural areas.

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: County commissioned formal planning study to address data center land use impacts as part of Comprehensive Plan update.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Local and Triangle media extensively covered organized community opposition to data center development.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Jordan Lake watershed concerns cited in official county documents opposing large data center water consumption.

scores.name: Dane County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Dane County (Madison) has active moratorium proposals and organized environmental opposition. Progressive political environment creates elevated risk of future restrictions.

signals.label: Moratorium Proposed
signals.description: Madison/Dane County policy discussions included formal moratorium proposals on new large data center construction following organized community opposition.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Progressive environmental and community groups organized campaign opposing data center expansion citing water, energy, and climate impact concerns.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County board hearings drew documented opposition testimony from environmental and community groups.

signals.label: Official Grid/Energy Concern
signals.description: Official county energy planning documents cited grid capacity concerns from proposed large data center loads.

scores.name: Boone County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Boone County enacted data center density restrictions after community opposition. Ongoing political pressure for further limits.

signals.label: Moratorium Enacted
signals.description: Boone County enacted restrictions and density limits on data center development following community opposition to rapid buildout in the Whitestown/Lebanon corridor.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Residents organized opposition to data center density growth, citing traffic, noise, and infrastructure strain.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County commissioner meetings drew documented public opposition to rezoning applications.

scores.name: Kalamazoo County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Kalamazoo County townships enacted moratoriums following documented organized opposition. Agricultural preservation concerns are the primary driver.

signals.label: Moratorium Enacted
signals.description: Township-level moratoriums on data center development enacted within Kalamazoo County following documented community opposition.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County and township planning hearings drew documented opposition testimony from residents citing agricultural character, noise, and water concerns.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Community groups organized opposition to proposed large data center facilities in Kalamazoo County townships.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Local media documented sustained community opposition to data center development proposals.

scores.name: Chelan County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Chelan County 'power wars' — organized community opposition to data centers' consumption of limited hydroelectric capacity. Formal policy restrictions enacted.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Local advocacy groups and utility customers organized against data centers consuming large shares of Chelan County PUD's low-cost hydroelectric capacity, displacing rate benefits for residents.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Multiple PUD and county commissioner hearings documented public opposition to continued data center buildout and power allocation.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Chelan County PUD and state regulators formally documented capacity constraints limiting further data center interconnections.

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: County commissioned review of data center land use impacts and power capacity limits as basis for future policy.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Extensive local and regional coverage of Chelan 'power wars' as residents and data centers competed for limited hydroelectric capacity.

scores.name: Douglas County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Douglas County faces organized opposition from PUD customers over hydroelectric capacity. Formal restrictions on new data center power connections documented.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Similar to Chelan County: Douglas County PUD customers organized opposition to data center dominance of hydroelectric capacity, raising rates and limiting capacity for other users.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: PUD rate hearings and county commissioner meetings drew documented public opposition to data center power allocation.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Douglas County PUD formally documented capacity constraints from data center load growth.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Regional media covered Douglas County power capacity concerns driven by data center load growth.

scores.name: Washoe County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Washoe County (Reno) has documented organized opposition driven by water scarcity in the Truckee River basin. Water authority capacity constraints and draft ordinances indicate elevated future restriction risk.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Reno-area residents and community groups organized documented opposition to data center water consumption amid Truckee River basin scarcity concerns.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County commissioner and planning commission hearings documented public opposition to data center water use in an arid high-desert basin.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Truckee Meadows Water Authority and Nevada Division of Water Resources formally documented water scarcity concerns affecting data center approvals.

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: Washoe County planning department initiated review of water-intensive data center land use as part of general plan update.

signals.label: Draft Ordinance Under Review
signals.description: Draft ordinance requiring water use efficiency standards and availability demonstrations for new large data center permits.

scores.name: Loudoun County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Loudoun County is in ongoing active political debate despite (or because of) being the world's largest data center market. Ongoing organized opposition targets rural expansion.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Western Loudoun rural preservation movement organized sustained campaign against data center expansion into agricultural zones; Rural Crescent Alliance active.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Ongoing public hearings on data center rezonings regularly draw documented opposition from western Loudoun residents and preservation advocates.

signals.label: Draft Ordinance Under Review
signals.description: Loudoun County adopted new overlay zones restricting data center development in western rural areas; further restrictions under ongoing policy review.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Sustained coverage in Northern Virginia and Washington DC media of community opposition to data center density in 'Data Center Alley.'

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: Loudoun County planning commission ongoing review of data center land use policy in context of General Plan update.

scores.name: Fairfax County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Fairfax County has documented organized opposition and enacted Comprehensive Plan restrictions. Ongoing community pressure for additional limits in western areas.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Western Fairfax community groups organized opposition to data center expansion in residential transition areas, citing noise, visual impact, and residential character.

signals.label: Draft Ordinance Under Review
signals.description: Fairfax County Board of Supervisors adopted Comprehensive Plan amendments restricting data center density in western districts; further restrictions under review.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Board of Supervisors meetings on data center rezonings draw documented community opposition in residential transition areas.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Northern Virginia and Washington DC media coverage of community opposition to data center encroachment in Fairfax residential areas.

scores.name: Prince William County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Prince William County's Digital Gateway controversy drove documented organized opposition. Political environment remains sensitive to data center expansion.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Digital Gateway community opposition group organized sustained campaign against data center density in the Manassas area, citing rural character and infrastructure concerns.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: Prince William County Board of Supervisors hearings on Digital Gateway data center rezonings drew documented organized opposition.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Sustained Northern Virginia media coverage of Digital Gateway controversy and community opposition.

scores.name: Spotsylvania County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: County board hearings on I-95 corridor data center developments drew documented opposition from residents citing noise, light pollution, and infrastructure concerns.

signals.label: Draft Ordinance Under Review
signals.description: Spotsylvania adopted Data Center Overlay District with density caps, noise ordinances, and renewable energy requirements following community pressure.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Local and Northern Virginia media covered community opposition driving the overlay district adoption.

scores.name: Maricopa County
scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.
scores.evidence_summary: Maricopa County faces strong water-driven political opposition backed by state regulatory action. Among the highest water-risk data center environments in the US.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Arizona Department of Water Resources formally required 100-year assured water supply demonstrations for Maricopa County data center projects; capacity constraints documented.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Arizona water advocacy groups organized sustained campaigns opposing data center water consumption in the drought-stressed CAP water system.

signals.label: Public Hearing — Opposition Documented
signals.description: State legislature and county hearings documented public opposition to data center water use amid Colorado River shortage.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Extensive statewide and national media coverage of Arizona water scarcity and data center opposition.

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: Maricopa County and state agencies initiated formal review of data center water use policy.

scores.score_description: High risk. Active political campaign, enacted or proposed moratoriums, organized advocacy groups, ban proposals, or election-issue status. High probability of future restrictions.

signals.label: Organized Political Campaign
signals.description: Community opposition to rapid data center development in rural Linn County documented; residents raised concerns about agricultural land conversion and water use.

signals.description: County planning and zoning hearings documented significant opposition testimony from rural residents.

signals.label: Planning Commission Study Ordered
signals.description: County initiated review of data center land use in rural areas; zoning ordinance updates under consideration.

signals.description: Putnam County community groups organized against proposed hyperscale data center facilities, part of the broader Indiana rural opposition movement.

signals.label: Local News Coverage of Opposition
signals.description: Local news coverage of community concerns about rapid growth and data center development impacts in Hays County's fast-growing communities.

signals.label: Official Water Concern
signals.description: Hays County and water district documents note groundwater availability concerns as population and data center growth increase demand.
